subject: Response formatting gap — propagate fix mesh-wide

body.context: Session 91 diagnosed why conversational response output lacked consistent formatting (APA tables, severity symbols, epistemic flags, golden ratio whitespace). Root cause: formatting conventions lived in glob-scoped rules files (*.md writes only), never reaching conversational output. Fix: add a Response Formatting section to CLAUDE.md so instructions load every session.

diagnosis.symptom: Agent responses lack consistent formatting — no APA tables, no severity symbols, no epistemic flags, inconsistent whitespace
diagnosis.root_cause: Formatting conventions in .claude/rules/markdown.md activate via globs: ["**/*.md"] — they fire on markdown FILE WRITES, not on conversational response composition. CLAUDE.md (loaded every session) had no formatting directives for response text.
diagnosis.scope: Affects all agents in the mesh. Any agent with formatting rules in glob-scoped files but not in CLAUDE.md exhibits the same gap.

fix.action: Add a Response Formatting section with the conventions that should apply to all conversational output
fix.content:
## Response Formatting

Apply to all conversational output (not just file writes):
- APA-style tables (no vertical rules) for structured data
- Symbols: ✓ ✗ ⚑ ⚠ for status; ██░░ HIGH, █░░░ MOD, ░░░░ LOW for severity
- Chunk, don't wall — offer stopping points for long outputs
- Golden ratio (1.618x) whitespace between logical sections
- Epistemic flags (⚑) in all substantive analytical output
- In-text citations (Author, Year) when connecting to established literature

propagation_recommendation.operations-agent: Add equivalent section to your CLAUDE.md. Adapt conventions to ops context — you may not need in-text citations but APA tables, severity symbols, and epistemic flags apply to mesh-wide reporting.
propagation_recommendation.all_agents: Any agent with formatting rules in .claude/rules/ but not in CLAUDE.md has this gap. The rules files serve file-write quality; CLAUDE.md serves response quality. Both needed.
propagation_recommendation.limitation: No hook enforcement possible — Claude Code lacks an assistant-output hook event type. Compliance depends on the model reading CLAUDE.md instructions. This represents a structural platform limitation, not a configuration gap.

claims.text: Glob-scoped rules files (.claude/rules/*.md) activate only on file operations matching the glob pattern, not on conversational response generation
claims.confidence_basis: Observed behavior across 91 sessions — rules fire on Write/Edit to matching files, never on response composition

epistemic_flags: The claim about glob activation scope derives from observed behavior, not from Claude Code documentation — the platform may change this behavior in future versions
